title: Add gold-standard curation workflow for feedback-backed labels for associated datasets
description:
## Summary
Add a gold-standard curation workflow that determines which feedback-backed labels are eligible for deterministic associated-dataset builds.

## Deliverables
- Prefer a dedicated review/curation model if schema budget allows after cleanup.
- If schema budget is constrained, this issue still owns the final storage-design decision rather than weakening the requirement informally.
- Define the workflow output that marks labels/items as eligible for associated-dataset inclusion.

## Dependency note
Depends on the schema-budget audit to confirm the preferred implementation path.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Terminology cleanup: this task now targets gold-standard curation for score-associated dataset builds. Scope is unchanged; only wording aligned with the association-only architecture decision.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Starting implementation. Immediate scope: define and ship a concrete gold-standard eligibility contract for feedback-backed labels and wire it into associated-dataset curation flow. Plan: (1) codify eligibility schema/output in shared guideline-vetting artifacts, (2) integrate eligibility filtering into score dataset-curate path, (3) add tests for selection determinism + eligibility enforcement, (4) update docs/help text and Kanbus progress notes.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Progress update: implemented explicit vetted-ID curation in the canonical score dataset-curate flow so gold-standard builds can be snapshot-bound to aligned-vetting output. Changes shipped: (1) score dataset-curate now accepts --feedback-item-ids (comma-separated) and routes to the explicit feedback-ID builder; (2) dashboard report-block API now passes vetted IDs to CLI via --feedback-item-ids instead of recomputing only by time window; (3) explicit-ID dataset builder now uses schema-compatible CreateDataSet input (removed unsupported provenance/buildContext fields). Validation: targeted tests passed (scores_dataset_curate_test + datasets_test subset, 6 passed).

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Runtime smoke test of explicit vetted-ID path executed with score dataset-curate --feedback-item-ids (scorecard 1039 / score 45425). Command reached row construction and failed at upload due environment prerequisite: AMPLIFY_STORAGE_DATASETS_BUCKET_NAME not configured in current shell. This is an environment blocker, not a command-path regression.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Code committed on feature branch: b36f61454. Documentation route hard-cut: /documentation/concepts/reference-datasets renamed to /documentation/concepts/associated-datasets, including sidebar and concepts landing links.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Progress update: added provenance pass-through for explicit vetted-ID builds in canonical score dataset-curate flow. New CLI options: --source-report-block-id and --eligibility-rule (valid only with --feedback-item-ids). Dashboard aligned report action now sends source_report_block_id + eligibility_rule to the associated-dataset API route, which forwards them to CLI. comments.author: ryan

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Minor terminology cleanup in active dashboard flow: task launch metadata now uses associated terms (type=dataset associated build, target=dataset/associated) instead of reference wording.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Runtime smoke test of new provenance options executed: score dataset-curate --feedback-item-ids ... --source-report-block-id block-456 --eligibility-rule "unanimous non-contradiction". Command path parsed options correctly and reached row construction; failure remained the known environment prerequisite (AMPLIFY_STORAGE_DATASETS_BUCKET_NAME missing) at upload stage.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Progress update: removed fallback behavior from dashboard associated-dataset build route. The route now requires explicit feedbackItemIds and always invokes score dataset-curate with --feedback-item-ids (snapshot-bound), plus optional source_report_block_id/eligibility_rule pass-through. Frontend aligned action updated to stop sending max/days fallback controls and to record task command/description as vetted snapshot curation. Tried targeted eslint on touched files, but local dashboard eslint currently fails in this environment with AJV/eslintrc runtime error (NOT SUPPORTED: option missingRefs).

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Docs update in progress: added explicit provenance options to associated-dataset curation examples (score dataset-curate --feedback-item-ids with --source-report-block-id and --eligibility-rule) in concepts/associated-datasets and methods/evaluate-score pages.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: End-to-end verification run completed on 2026-04-03 UTC. Set AMPLIFY_STORAGE_DATASETS_BUCKET_NAME from dashboard/amplify_outputs.json and executed snapshot-bound build + associated evaluation. Dataset curate command succeeded with explicit vetted ID/provenance: dataset_id=10e1d33b-863e-4d42-8ceb-e1f84a37444f, row_count=1, feedback_item_count=1, eligibility_rule="unanimous non-contradiction", source_report_block_id=block-456. Follow-up accuracy evaluation against latest associated dataset succeeded: evaluation_id=72b20c4d-f8f4-4fd9-a81d-9ddbee521103, status=COMPLETED, processedItems=1, totalItems=1, accuracy=0.0.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Practical-scale run executed after 1-item plumbing check. Built associated dataset from 180-day window with max-items=100: dataset_id=c09c5f03-f957-4b9f-90e3-495af0f7f26f, qualifying_found=100, rows_written=100. Started associated-dataset accuracy evaluations: (a) eval 807552e6-6361-4eef-9612-a52b4064b667 on 100 samples currently RUNNING with 39/100 processed (updatedAt 2026-04-03T00:16:29.806Z, accuracy 41.0256 so far); (b) eval 915b738a-0f4c-4cb8-b638-e6e91a12981b on 20 samples currently SETUP pending worker availability.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Planned sub-task split approved: PR #78 gate first, then champion-class resolver, balanced curation, persisted dataset stats, score-page associated datasets UI, docs, and runtime validation.

Implementation assumptions locked:
- no fallback paths
- no schema migration required for PR #78 validation contract itself
- class source for balancing is champion score YAML
- balancing default is on; --no-balance is explicit opt-out

Dependency chain:
plx-82a3aa -> plx-01fa70 -> plx-072c43 -> plx-9402ab -> plx-9fc883
plx-072c43 -> plx-eca6cb
plx-9fc883 + plx-eca6cb -> plx-ea7193

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Design adjustment for active implementation: class-list resolution currently hard-requires champion YAML, which blocks non-champion iteration. Implementing one explicit path for dataset-curate balancing: optional --score-version-id to source class contract from that exact score version; if omitted, use champion. No fallback paths beyond this explicit selector.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implemented non-champion class-source support for associated dataset curation balancing. Changes: (1) class resolver generalized to resolve_score_valid_classes_from_score_yaml with optional score_version_id; when provided, class extraction uses that exact version (no champion lookup), otherwise champion is used. (2) score dataset-curate now accepts --score-version-id and passes it into window-curation balancing path. (3) validation added: --score-version-id cannot be combined with --feedback-item-ids (explicit-ID path does not use balancing classes). Tests: pytest -q plexus/cli/dataset/curation_test.py plexus/cli/score/scores_dataset_curate_test.py -q => 17 passed.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Runtime validation complete for non-champion class source path. Using --score-version-id with score dataset-curate succeeded end-to-end on scorecard 1039 / score 45425. Command: score dataset-curate --scorecard 1039 --score 45425 --score-version-id 95db98ae-bcec-4cd2-8a6e-3025d4056371 --max-items 25 --days 180. Result: dataset_id=2ddea930-4a6d-428f-b44c-55a5a0e6c528, qualifying_found=6888, rows_written=25, balance_applied=true.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implementation kickoff for final-class balancing fix. Root cause confirmed: resolver collected graph-wide intermediate node classes (e.g., compliance reason categories) instead of final score output classes, causing balancing mismatch on Yes/No datasets. Architecture decision: final-output-only class resolution (ordered sources: parameters.validation.value.valid_classes -> top-level classes -> final graph node valid_classes -> final graph node conditions outputs -> static extraction from final LogicalClassifier code) + fail-fast on unusably low class/label overlap for balancing. Acceptance target: scorecard 1039 / score 45425 resolves Yes/No from non-champion score version and persists resolver diagnostics in dataset_stats/CLI output.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implemented final-output-only class resolver + fail-fast overlap contract and validated runtime behavior. Validation summary: score dataset-curate --scorecard 1039 --score 45425 --score-version-id 95db98ae-bcec-4cd2-8a6e-3025d4056371 --max-items 25 --days 180 created dataset 578df803-f093-4ba5-9cc1-f474ea64cadb. Persisted dataset_stats now include class_resolution_source=graph[-1].LogicalClassifier.code, resolved_final_classes=[Yes, No], observed_label_set=[NA, No, Yes], class_label_overlap=[No, Yes]. Distribution moved from before {No:7, Yes:18} to after {No:12, Yes:13}. Targeted tests passed: pytest -q plexus/cli/dataset/curation_test.py plexus/cli/score/scores_dataset_curate_test.py -q (20 passed).

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Sub-task split added for this implementation: plx-4ada08 (CLI orchestration), plx-3b9e2c (report config auto-management), plx-1ae746 (eligible payload ordering), plx-1d0986 (vetted+balanced builder), plx-52e63c (UI/API route removal), plx-1973d9 (docs). Beginning implementation now in this dependency order: report payload + builder + CLI orchestration + UI cleanup + docs.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Completed automated vetted+balanced associated dataset pipeline (report-evidence-first) and runtime validation.

Runtime validation (2026-04-03 UTC, scorecard 1039 / score 45425):
- Command: plexus score dataset-curate-vetted --scorecard 1039 --score 45425 --days 180 --max-items 100 --score-version-id 95db98ae-bcec-4cd2-8a6e-3025d4056371
- Report configuration: b9aa195a-99f3-42f9-9f69-bc314bcc3dfd
- Report ID: 30d4c0eb-06ef-47de-ac9c-5292ea71f935
- Report block ID: 28b841ea-b170-43b2-ad7a-6e35cdce279a
- Report task ID: b0b66641-13bf-4fe4-96c5-8fff5f2f4fbe
- Dataset ID: 94e5d76d-7979-4526-a8c5-4a5d4ef7123d
- Rows written: 51 (vetted_eligible_count=51, selected_vetted_count=51)
- Final classes: [Yes, No] via graph[-1].LogicalClassifier.code
- Distribution before/after balancing: {No:13, Yes:38} -> {No:13, Yes:38}

Operational fix applied for reliability/performance in this path:
- Added deterministic max_feedback_items cap in FeedbackContradictions aligned flow.
- Enforced cap at feedback fetch pagination layer (newest-first) to avoid scanning full history when capped.
- Auto-managed report config now sets max_feedback_items to requested max-items for predictable runtime/cost.
- No fallback paths introduced.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Hotfix: resolved dashboard runtime ReferenceError in FeedbackContradictions block (Button is not defined). Cause: missing Button import after UI action cleanup. Fix: added import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button' in dashboard/components/blocks/FeedbackContradictions.tsx. Verified by code inspection; local eslint remains blocked by known AJV/eslintrc environment error.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Post-completion UX tweak: in FeedbackContradictions aligned mode, removed per-item invalidation control. Mark Invalid button (and invalidation status badge) now render only in contradictions mode; aligned mode remains read-only evidence view.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: UI tweak: vote badge polarity now depends on mode. In aligned/agreement mode, green now indicates agreement/aligned vote and red indicates disagreement/contradiction vote; contradiction mode retains existing polarity.
